name: ownership transfer cannot move the owner edge off the prior owner
description: NEW GAP behaviour (related to but distinct from C1/H2/reschedule/role-reference). 'I gave my old bike to Otto' should MOVE the owner edge: close Me.owns->Bike and open Otto.owns->Bike, leaving exactly one active owner. But supersession is per identity key (subject, entity, predicate, qualifier) and the new fact's entity_ref is Otto -- a DIFFERENT key from Me.owns. The pipeline has no path to supersede a fact belonging to another subject's key, so Me.owns stays active AND Otto.owns is inserted: the bike now has two simultaneous owners. The model emitting only the Otto edge (the natural extraction) cannot express the transfer. Spec-correct (docs/ANALYSIS.md 'property graph': single current owner for a transferred chattel): Me.owns must be closed/superseded. xfail until the pipeline can close a counterparty's edge on transfer (would require the extraction to also emit a negated Me.owns AND the dispose path to work).
xfail: owner edge cannot move across subjects: decide() resolves per (subject,entity,predicate,qualifier) so the new Otto.owns key never sees the Me.owns head -- prior owner stays active (pipeline._existing_facts scopes to one entity)

steps.body: I own an old mountain bike.

facts.statement: I own an old mountain bike.

steps.body: Gave my old bike to Otto.

facts.statement: Otto owns the old bike now.
